title: A Skill-Mediated LLM Workflow for Migrating a .NET Monolith to Microservices: Reproducibility Deposit
description: Reproducibility artefacts accompanying the IEEE Access manuscript by Olariu and Alboaie. Contains: ten Claude Code skill scripts (pinned by SHA-256), the PetRescue and eShopOnWeb case-study codebases tagged at the experimental commit, raw run logs and transcripts for ten skill-mediated /refactor runs and ten baseline runs on PetRescue and three of each on eShopOnWeb, the security injection corpus (ten labelled vulnerable specimens and ten matched clean specimens), the pre-registered twenty-dimension structural rubrics for both cases, the dependency-free Python scoring and inferential-statistics scripts, the verifier changelog, and a full environment manifest (LLM model identity, Claude Code version, hardware, .NET SDK, Docker, all skill SHAs, all slice commits). Re-running the harness against the same model identity (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929) should reproduce the qualitative pattern reported in the paper within the standard deviations reported there.

creators.name: Olariu, Florin
creators.affiliation: Faculty of Computer Science, Alexandru Ioan Cuza University of Iasi, Romania; Suvoda LLC

creators.name: Alboaie, Lenuta
creators.affiliation: Faculty of Computer Science, Alexandru Ioan Cuza University of Iasi, Romania

notes: Companion deposit for IEEE Access submission. The manuscript is not yet peer-reviewed at the time of deposit; this record will be updated with the DOI of the published article on acceptance.
